seoTitle: How to Block Outlook Calendar for Out of Office (2026)
metaDescription: Reserve time off in Outlook by creating an out-of-office calendar block, marking your status, and auto-declining meeting invites while you're away.
intro: Leaving your calendar wide open while you're away can lead to double-booked meetings and unwanted invites. Outlook lets you create a dedicated away block, mark your availability status, and even auto-decline incoming requests, so your team knows exactly when not to schedule you.

steps.heading: Create a new appointment for your time away
steps.body: Open your Outlook calendar and start a new event covering the exact dates you'll be unavailable, whether it's a single day or a longer stretch.

steps.heading: Set your status to Out of Office
steps.body: Use the Show As dropdown on the event to change your availability tag so coworkers see you as unavailable rather than free or busy.

steps.heading: Choose all-day or specific hours
steps.body: Check the all-day event option, or set precise start and end times if you're only away for part of the day.

steps.heading: Enable auto-decline for conflicting invites
steps.body: Turn on the setting that automatically declines new meeting requests that land during your blocked time, so you don't have to manually reject them.

steps.heading: Save and confirm the block displays
steps.body: Save the event and check your calendar view to make sure the out-of-office block appears clearly to anyone viewing your shared schedule.

faq.question: Will an out-of-office calendar block automatically decline meetings?
faq.answer: Only if you enable the auto-decline option on the event; otherwise it just visually marks you as unavailable without rejecting invites automatically.

faq.question: Can I set up a recurring out-of-office block for regular time off?
faq.answer: Yes, you can build a recurring pattern for regular vacation days or seasonal breaks instead of creating a new event each time.

faq.question: Does blocking my calendar stop people from emailing me?
faq.answer: No, this only affects your calendar and meeting invites. You'd need to set up a separate automatic reply for emails.

faq.question: Can I mark only part of a day as out of office?
faq.answer: Yes, instead of checking the all-day option you can set specific start and end times for a partial-day block.
